asset_name: World backdrop — sepia sky gradient fill
shared_properties: The layer covers the entire 1280x720 frame and supports the warm autumn palette.
The backdrop stays non-interactive and sits behind every gameplay collider.
The lower area remains slightly darker to improve player and obstacle readability.
differences: This base layer establishes the sepia sky gradient that defines the game mood instantly.
The gradient brightens near the top edge to suggest open air before the heavier canopy takes over.
The lower fall corridor becomes subtly darker so obstacles and the player leaf remain easy to scan.

reference_images.label: leaf_beat_gdd
reference_images.purpose: Grounds the core loop, timing windows, obstacle pacing, UI layout, and Bevy target constraints.

reference_images.label: leaf_beat_moodboard
reference_images.purpose: Grounds the sepia autumn atmosphere, tree silhouettes, and warm color layering.

bevy_constraints.bundle_strategy: SpriteBundle full-screen backdrop entity rendered behind every gameplay and UI layer.
bevy_constraints.asset_loading: Load world/art_000011.png during boot and keep the handle resident for every gameplay state that shows the falling stage.
runtime_notes: Render this layer at the farthest depth with no parallax offset so camera movement never reveals empty space.
Keep all gradient bands hand-authored in the texture rather than generated at runtime to preserve captured footage consistency.
Use nearest-neighbor sampling and whole-pixel placement even though the gradient is soft so edge seams never appear on upscale.

context.usage: Full-screen background layer that establishes the warm autumn sky across the whole run.

shape.silhouette: Full-screen vertical gradient with a bright upper band and a darker lower fall corridor.

acceptance_criteria: PNG canvas is exactly 1280x720px with no transparent pixels inside the active frame.
The top 120 pixels stay visibly brighter than the lower 200 pixels to support depth perception during descent.
No band edge creates a hard contour that could be misread as gameplay geometry.
